polish.worst_offense: The speedometer icon inside the green circle at time ~4s reads as decorative clip-art, violating the no-emoji/icon-in-drawing-surface rule and creating salience noise.
fixes: Remove the compass/speedometer icon from inside the teal circle marker; replace with a simple dot or small crosshair if a check-point is needed, or leave the circle unfilled to mark the sampled point by outline alone.

Verify the purple 'b' handle at t=8s has a 24px+ hit area and exhibits hover scale-up (~1.15×, spring-eased) with subtle shadow; the current affordance is borderline and should be more inviting to signal draggability.
Confirm the 'Est: 0.0' readout uses tabular numerals so it doesn't reflow width as the estimate changes during interaction—currently it may jitter horizontally as the user adjusts the drag window.

issues: The variable `b` appears to control vertical position/offset of the curve rather than student-initiated dragging; the design specifies student manipulation via drag interaction, not parameter animation
No color change in shaded regions is visible across frames to distinguish positive vs. negative area contributions—all shading appears uniform, contradicting the core visual feedback mechanism
The integral value label and axis markers remain static across frames despite the curve shifting, suggesting annotations are not properly tracking the curve's position
The interactive dragging mechanism shown in the design is absent; the filmstrip shows automated parameter variation rather than interactive student control with real-time feedback

The variable `n` appears to control the amplitude or shape of the curve rather than a vertical offset/dragging position; the curve oscillates differently across frames instead of maintaining consistent shape while shifting vertically

The green and blue endpoint markers remain fixed on the x-axis across all frames while the curve changes shape, breaking the expected geometric relationship where dragging should move the entire curve together

The integral value displayed changes across frames due to curve shape variation, not due to signed area behavior from vertical repositioning; this obscures the intended causal chain between position and signed area
No interactive dragging mechanic is visible in the filmstrip; the frames show automatic parametric variation rather than student-controlled vertical offset as specified in the design intent

visual_check_failures.expected: curve crosses x-axis, showing both teal (above) and coral (below) shaded regions; net area readout visible
visual_check_failures.observed: No visible changes between the before and after screenshots; both appear identical with the same speed curve, timeline, and interface elements.

visual_check_failures.expected: curve shifts upward, coral region shrinks significantly, more of the curve is above x-axis
visual_check_failures.observed: The curve and all visual elements remain identical between the two screenshots; no visible shift or change occurred.

visual_check_failures.expected: more coral shading below x-axis, net area readout shows negative value
visual_check_failures.observed: The airplane icon moved slightly to the right along the curve and is now circled with a teal outline, but no coral shading appeared below the x-axis and no negative value is visible in the readout.

visual_check_failures.expected: both teal and coral regions visible with similar magnitudes
visual_check_failures.observed: The cursor/sampling point moved slightly to the right on the timeline (from approximately 4.0 to 4.2 seconds), but no teal and coral regions are visible in either screenshot.

visual_check_failures.expected: integration bounds a and b shown as fixed markers on the x-axis
visual_check_failures.observed: The speedometer icon moved slightly along the curve, but the integration bounds markers (a and b) remain in the same positions on the x-axis in both images.

results.description: On load, the curve has both positive and negative regions visible, posing the signed area question
results.visual_check: curve crosses x-axis, showing both teal (above) and coral (below) shaded regions; net area readout visible

visual_verdict.observed: No visible changes between the before and after screenshots; both appear identical with the same speed curve, timeline, and interface elements.

results.description: The vertical offset can be adjusted by dragging the curve or using a slider
results.visual_check: curve shifts upward, coral region shrinks significantly, more of the curve is above x-axis

visual_verdict.observed: The curve and all visual elements remain identical between the two screenshots; no visible shift or change occurred.

results.description: When the curve dips below the axis, the negative region subtracts from the net area
results.visual_check: more coral shading below x-axis, net area readout shows negative value

visual_verdict.observed: The airplane icon moved slightly to the right along the curve and is now circled with a teal outline, but no coral shading appeared below the x-axis and no negative value is visible in the readout.

results.description: At a specific offset, positive and negative areas cancel to give net zero
results.visual_check: both teal and coral regions visible with similar magnitudes

visual_verdict.observed: The cursor/sampling point moved slightly to the right on the timeline (from approximately 4.0 to 4.2 seconds), but no teal and coral regions are visible in either screenshot.

results.description: The parameters a, b, and n from previous sections are displayed but locked
results.visual_check: integration bounds a and b shown as fixed markers on the x-axis

visual_verdict.observed: The speedometer icon moved slightly along the curve, but the integration bounds markers (a and b) remain in the same positions on the x-axis in both images.
